articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Phase II Unveiled, India Aims for Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and export capabilities.
• New incentives for green hydrogen production, manufacturing of electrolysers, and development of green hydrogen hubs were announced.
• The mission aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors, and create significant employment opportunities.
• Emphasis is placed on R&D for cost reduction and efficient storage, alongside international collaborations for technology transfer.
• This phase targets achieving 10 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030, positioning India as a global leader.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission approved.
Target: 10 million tonnes annual green hydrogen production by 2030.
Focus on production incentives, electrolyser manufacturing, green hydrogen hubs.
Aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and export.
Evaluate the role of government incentives and R&D in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential for job creation and industrial diversification through the Green Hydrogen Mission.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Gopichand Hinduja, Chairman of Hinduja Group, Dies at 85
articles.explainer:
• Gopichand Hinduja, the patriarch and chairman of the global Hinduja Group, passed away at the age of 85.
• He, along with his brothers, significantly expanded the diversified conglomerate founded by their father, Parmanand Deepchand Hinduja.
• The Hinduja Group has interests spanning automotive, oil and gas, banking and finance, IT, power, media, and real estate globally, with a strong presence in India.
• He was instrumental in establishing the group's presence in various sectors, contributing significantly to India's economic landscape through investments and employment.
• His legacy includes building a vast business empire and promoting philanthropic initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Gopichand Hinduja, Chairman of Hinduja Group, passed away.
Hinduja Group is a diversified global conglomerate.
Key sectors include automotive, finance, IT, power, real estate.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of large conglomerates like the Hinduja Group in India's economic development and global integration.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for family-run businesses in maintaining global competitiveness and adapting to changing economic landscapes.
Examine the impact of philanthropic initiatives by Indian business leaders on social development.
articles.trivia: The Hinduja Group was founded in 1914 in Shikarpur, Sindh (now in Pakistan), and initially traded in carpets, dried fruits, and saffron.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Review of Data Protection Bill Provisions on State Surveillance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, directed the government to review certain provisions of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023.
• The court emphasized the need for clearer safeguards against potential state surveillance, citing concerns over the balance between national security and individual privacy.
• The ruling stemmed from petitions challenging clauses that grant broad exemptions to government agencies from data processing obligations.
• The bench highlighted the 'right to privacy' as a fundamental right and mandated a more robust oversight mechanism for data access by state entities.
• This decision is expected to lead to amendments ensuring greater accountability and transparency in state data handling practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Cyber security challenges
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruled on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023.
Directed review of provisions related to state surveillance exemptions.
Emphasized 'right to privacy' and need for robust oversight.
Impacts government data handling and transparency.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the balance between national security and individual privacy in the digital age.
Discuss the challenges in drafting data protection laws that effectively safeguard citizen rights while allowing for legitimate state functions.
Evaluate the role of judicial review in upholding fundamental rights against legislative overreach in the context of emerging technologies.
Suggest legislative and technological measures to enhance data protection and minimize surveillance risks.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books in a courtroom setting.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes, Boosting Digital Cooperation and Trade Ties
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to strengthening strategic partnership.
• Key agreements were reached on enhancing digital cooperation, including cybersecurity, digital payments, and developing a regional digital economy framework.
• Discussions also focused on accelerating trade and investment flows, with a renewed push for reviewing the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA).
• Regional security challenges, including maritime security and counter-terrorism, were also addressed, emphasizing collaborative approaches.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic alignment with Southeast Asian nations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Summit concluded with focus on digital cooperation and trade.
Agreements on cybersecurity, digital payments, and regional digital economy.
Discussion on reviewing ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA).
Reaffirms India's 'Act East' policy and strategic partnership.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of India's 'Act East' policy in strengthening ties with ASEAN nations in the current geopolitical scenario.
Discuss the potential of digital cooperation between India and ASEAN in fostering economic growth and addressing regional challenges.
Analyze the role of trade agreements like AIFTA in boosting economic integration and what reforms are needed for its effective implementation.
Examine the shared security concerns between India and ASEAN and strategies for collaborative approaches.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands at a summit.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Aditya-L2 Mission for Advanced Solar Studies
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Aditya-L2 mission, India's second dedicated solar observatory.
• Aditya-L2 aims to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the Sun's atmosphere, solar winds, and coronal mass ejections (CMEs).
• The spacecraft will be placed in a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1), offering an uninterrupted view of the Sun.
• Equipped with advanced payloads, it will study solar dynamics, space weather, and their impact on Earth's communication systems.
• This mission reinforces India's growing capabilities in space science and its commitment to contributing to global scientific knowledge.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Aditya-L2 is India's second dedicated solar observatory.
Will be placed in a halo orbit around Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1).
Aims to study solar atmosphere, solar winds, CMEs, and space weather.
Reinforces ISRO's capabilities in space science.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and significance of India's solar missions, such as Aditya-L1 and Aditya-L2, for understanding space weather and its impact.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to India's scientific prowess and global standing.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to study the Sun and the technological innovations required.
Elaborate on the concept of Lagrange Points and their importance in space-based observatories.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Aditya-L2 spacecraft orbiting the Sun at L1 point.
articles.trivia: Lagrange Points are positions in space where the gravitational forces of two large bodies (like the Sun and Earth) balance each other, providing stable locations for spacecraft.

articles.headline: Union Government Unveils New Comprehensive Wetlands Conservation Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new national policy aimed at strengthening wetland conservation and management.
• The policy emphasizes a participatory approach, involving local communities and stakeholders in the restoration and sustainable use of wetlands.
• It introduces stricter regulations against encroachment and pollution, alongside provisions for ecological restoration and biodiversity protection.
• Special focus is given to urban wetlands, recognizing their critical role in flood mitigation, water purification, and urban biodiversity.
• The policy aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and aims to expand the network of Ramsar Sites.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New national wetlands conservation policy launched.
Emphasizes participatory approach and stricter regulations.
Focus on urban wetlands and alignment with Ramsar Convention.
Aims to expand Ramsar Sites network.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Critically analyze the features of the new wetlands conservation policy and its potential effectiveness in addressing these threats.
Examine the challenges in implementing participatory conservation models and strategies to overcome them.
Elaborate on India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and how this policy strengthens them.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in 1971 in the Iranian city of Ramsar.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focuses on Inflation Control Amid Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance amidst persistent global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI Governor stated that the focus remains on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The central bank highlighted concerns over core inflation stickiness and geopolitical risks impacting commodity prices.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with future rate decisions heavily dependent on evolving inflation trajectory and global cues.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Focus on inflation control and withdrawing accommodation.
Concerns over core inflation and geopolitical risks.
Sixth consecutive meeting with unchanged rates.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in managing inflation and ensuring economic stability in India.
Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst global economic uncertainties and domestic pressures.
Evaluate the impact of high inflation on various sectors of the Indian economy and the strategies adopted by the government and RBI to mitigate it.
Examine the trade-offs between controlling inflation and promoting economic growth in the context of monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with Indian currency notes in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Modernization Drive: Indian Army Inducts Advanced Indigenous Artillery Systems
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Army formally inducted a new batch of indigenously developed advanced artillery gun systems, bolstering its combat capabilities.
• These systems, designed and manufactured by Indian firms, demonstrate significant progress in 'Make in India' in defense.
• The induction aims to replace aging artillery and enhance firepower, range, and precision in varied terrains, including mountainous regions.
• This move aligns with the government's push for self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in defense production and reducing import dependence.
• Training programs for personnel have commenced to ensure seamless integration and operational readiness of the new equipment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Indian Army inducted new indigenous advanced artillery gun systems.
Part of 'Make in India' and Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence.
Aims to enhance firepower, range, and precision.
Replaces aging equipment and reduces import dependence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous defence production for India's national security and strategic autonomy.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in critical defence technologies and manufacturing.
Evaluate the impact of such modernizations on India's conventional deterrence capabilities and border security.
Examine the economic benefits of the 'Make in India' initiative in the defence sector, including job creation and technological spin-offs.
articles.imageDescription: An indigenous artillery gun system being test-fired in a desert environment.
articles.trivia: The Dhanush artillery gun, an upgraded version of the Bofors gun, was the first indigenously produced long-range artillery gun in India.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Skill India Plus' Initiative for Future-Ready Workforce
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Skill India Plus', an enhanced version of the existing Skill India Mission.
• This new initiative focuses on upskilling and reskilling the workforce in emerging technologies like AI, IoT, blockchain, and green skills.
• It aims to bridge the skill gap in industries undergoing rapid technological transformation and boost employability among youth.
• Key features include industry-academia partnerships, digital learning platforms, and greater emphasis on apprenticeship opportunities.
• The program targets creating a dynamic and adaptable workforce capable of meeting the demands of the 21st-century global economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Skill India Plus initiative launched by Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
Focus on upskilling/reskilling in emerging technologies (AI, IoT, green skills).
Aims to bridge skill gaps and boost youth employability.
Features industry-academia partnerships and digital learning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of initiatives like 'Skill India Plus' in leveraging India's demographic dividend and preparing its workforce for future challenges.
Analyze the role of emerging technologies in transforming the job market and the implications for skill development policies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of previous skill development programs and suggest measures for improving the outreach and impact of 'Skill India Plus'.
Examine the importance of industry-academia collaboration and apprenticeship models in creating a job-ready workforce.
articles.imageDescription: Young people engaged in vocational training using modern technology.
articles.trivia: The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008 as a not-for-profit public limited company under Section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Bonds Scheme for Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The committee emphasized the need for greater transparency in political funding and suggested mechanisms to disclose donor identities to ensure accountability.
• Concerns were raised regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on fair electoral practices and corporate influence.
• Recommendations include lowering the minimum denomination of electoral bonds and introducing a digital portal for real-time disclosure of donations.
• The report calls for a comprehensive review of electoral finance laws to enhance public trust in democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Transparency & Accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary panel recommended reforms to Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Suggested disclosing donor identities for greater transparency.
Concerns over anonymity and corporate influence.
Recommendations include lowering denomination and digital disclosure.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bonds Scheme in India, highlighting its objectives and the controversies surrounding it.
Discuss the importance of transparency in political funding for a healthy democracy and the challenges in achieving it.
Evaluate the recommendations made by the Parliamentary Standing Committee and their potential impact on electoral reforms.
Examine alternative models of political funding adopted in other democracies and their applicability to India.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with a focus on governance.
articles.trivia: Electoral bonds were introduced in 2017 through the Finance Bill, 2017, amending the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

articles.headline: India and UK Sign Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement
articles.explainer:
• India and the United Kingdom officially signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) after extensive negotiations.
• The agreement aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment across various sectors, including services, manufacturing, and technology.
• Key provisions include tariff reductions, easier market access, and cooperation on intellectual property rights and sustainable trade practices.
• This CEPA is expected to open new avenues for Indian businesses in the UK market and attract British investments into India.
• Both nations anticipate a substantial increase in bilateral trade, contributing to economic growth and job creation in both economies.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and UK signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA).
Aims to boost bilateral trade and investment.
Includes tariff reductions, market access, IP cooperation.
Expected to increase bilateral trade substantially.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the India-UK CEPA for India's trade diversification and economic growth post-Brexit.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges for key Indian sectors, such as textiles, pharmaceuticals, and services, from this agreement.
Evaluate the role of CEPA in strengthening India's geopolitical and economic ties with major global powers.
Examine how such trade agreements contribute to job creation and technological exchange between partner countries.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together.
articles.trivia: The UK is one of the largest investors in India among G7 countries, while India is the second-largest investor in the UK.

articles.headline: Indian Researchers Achieve Breakthrough in Low-Cost Carbon Capture Technology
articles.explainer:
• Scientists at an Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) announced a significant breakthrough in developing a novel, low-cost carbon capture technology.
• The new method utilizes advanced materials and processes that are more energy-efficient and scalable compared to existing technologies.
• This innovation has the potential to drastically reduce the cost of capturing carbon dioxide emissions from industrial sources.
• The technology is crucial for India, a major emitter, to meet its climate targets and transition towards a net-zero economy.
• Further research and pilot projects are underway to demonstrate its commercial viability and widespread application.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: IIT researchers developed a low-cost carbon capture technology.
Utilizes energy-efficient and scalable advanced materials.
Aims to reduce CO2 emissions from industrial sources.
Crucial for India's climate targets and net-zero transition.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technologies in achieving India's climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up CCUS technologies in India, considering cost, infrastructure, and public acceptance.
Evaluate the potential impact of indigenous technological breakthroughs like this on India's industrial decarbonization efforts.
Examine the policy support and investment required to transition from lab-scale innovation to widespread commercial deployment of green technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Laboratory equipment symbolizing scientific research in carbon capture.
articles.trivia: The first industrial-scale carbon capture plant was built in 1938 at a chemical plant in Texas, USA, to produce CO2 for beverage carbonation.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavation revealed stupas, monastic cells, statues of Buddhist deities, and intricate carvings dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This finding adds significant new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its architectural and artistic traditions.
• The site is believed to be part of the historical 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist heritage in Odisha, which includes Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri.
• Efforts are underway for further exploration, preservation, and potential development of the site as a heritage tourism destination.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient Buddhist monastery in Jajpur, Odisha.
Dates back to 7th-10th centuries CE.
Revealed stupas, monastic cells, Buddhist deities, carvings.
Adds to the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist heritage in Odisha.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history of Buddhism and its influence on Indian art and culture.
Analyze the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Examine the potential of heritage sites like this to boost cultural tourism and local economic development in regions like Odisha.
Elaborate on the architectural features and iconography typically found in ancient Buddhist monastic complexes in India.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with stupas and statues.
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri) is known for its rich Buddhist archaeological remains, reflecting the growth of Vajrayana Buddhism.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Organic Farming and Agri-Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare introduced a new policy framework aimed at promoting organic farming and increasing agri-exports.
• The policy provides enhanced financial support, technical assistance, and market linkages for farmers transitioning to organic practices.
• It emphasizes certification mechanisms, quality control, and brand building for Indian organic products in domestic and international markets.
• The initiative seeks to improve soil health, reduce chemical input use, and provide better income opportunities for farmers.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to sustainable agriculture and tapping into the growing global demand for organic produce.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, – different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Public Distribution System– objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: New policy framework launched for organic farming and agri-exports.
Provides financial support, technical assistance, market linkages.
Focus on certification, quality control, brand building.
Aims to improve soil health, reduce chemical use, increase farmer income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the benefits and challenges of promoting organic farming in India, considering food security and farmer livelihoods.
Analyze how the new policy framework can contribute to doubling farmers' income and enhancing India's position in global organic markets.
Examine the role of government support, technological interventions, and market infrastructure in developing a robust organic agriculture sector.
Evaluate the environmental implications of widespread adoption of organic farming practices in India.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a lush organic field with healthy crops.
articles.trivia: Sikkim became India's first fully organic state in 2016, converting all its farmland to certified organic.

articles.headline: National Commission for Women Proposes Stricter Laws Against Cyberstalking
articles.explainer:
• The National Commission for Women (NCW) submitted recommendations to the Ministry of Home Affairs for amending existing laws to combat cyberstalking more effectively.
• The proposals include increasing penalties for offenders, expanding the definition of cyberstalking to cover emerging digital harassment forms, and streamlining reporting mechanisms.
• The NCW highlighted the rising incidence of online harassment, particularly against women and children, and its severe psychological and social impact.
• Emphasis was placed on enhancing digital literacy and awareness campaigns to empower victims and prevent such crimes.
• The recommendations aim to create a safer online environment and ensure justice for victims of cybercrimes.
gsSubTopics: Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Cyber security challenges
prelimsPoints: NCW proposed stricter laws against cyberstalking.
Recommendations include increased penalties and broader definition.
Aims to combat online harassment against women and children.
Emphasizes digital literacy and awareness campaigns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing menace of cyberstalking and online harassment in India, particularly its impact on women and vulnerable groups.
Critically analyze the existing legal framework for addressing cybercrimes and the need for reforms as suggested by the NCW.
Examine the multi-faceted approach required, including legal, technological, and social measures, to create a safer online environment.
Evaluate the role of statutory bodies like the NCW in advocating for gender justice and policy changes in India.
articles.imageDescription: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone with digital threat icons hovering around.
articles.trivia: The Information Technology Act, 2000, is the primary law in India dealing with cybercrime and e-commerce.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Develop Novel Vaccine for Neglected Tropical Disease
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists announced the successful development of a novel vaccine candidate for a prevalent neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The vaccine, developed after years of research, showed promising results in preclinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• This breakthrough holds significant potential for public health in regions disproportionately affected by NTDs, which often receive less research funding.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitment to global health and its 'Vaccine Maitri' diplomacy, showcasing its indigenous R&D capabilities.
• Clinical trials are expected to commence soon, paving the way for a cost-effective solution to a critical health challenge.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Indian scientists developed a novel vaccine for a neglected tropical disease (NTD).
Showed promising results in preclinical trials.
Significant for public health in NTD-affected regions.
Aligns with India's global health commitments and 'Vaccine Maitri'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and globally, and the challenges in their control and elimination.
Analyze the significance of indigenous vaccine development in strengthening India's public health infrastructure and its role as a global vaccine hub.
Evaluate the ethical considerations and regulatory pathways involved in bringing a new vaccine from research to widespread public use.
Examine the 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its impact on India's soft power and international relations.
articles.imageDescription: Microscope and test tubes in a laboratory setting, symbolizing medical research.
articles.trivia: Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) are a diverse group of communicable diseases that prevail in tropical and subtropical conditions in 149 countries and affect more than one billion people.

articles.headline: Election Commission of India Launches Nationwide Electoral Roll Digitization Project
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) initiated a massive project to digitize and modernize electoral rolls across the country.
• The project aims to enhance accuracy, accessibility, and transparency of voter lists, reducing errors and ensuring wider participation.
• It involves advanced data analytics, GIS mapping, and integration with Aadhaar (voluntary) for de-duplication and verification.
• The ECI emphasized the importance of stakeholder consultations and robust data security measures to protect voter information.
• This initiative is a significant step towards improving electoral integrity and streamlining the electoral process in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: ECI launched nationwide electoral roll digitization project.
Aims for accurate, accessible, transparent voter lists.
Uses data analytics, GIS mapping, voluntary Aadhaar integration.
Focus on data security and stakeholder consultation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of accurate and updated electoral rolls for the integrity of democratic elections in India.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of digitizing electoral rolls, including issues of data privacy and accessibility for all sections of society.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India in upholding the fairness and transparency of the electoral process through such reforms.
Examine how technology can be leveraged to enhance voter participation and reduce electoral malpractices in a diverse country like India.
articles.imageDescription: An electronic voting machine (EVM) and voter ID cards, symbolizing electoral process.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a permanent and independent body established by the Constitution of India directly to ensure free and fair elections in the country.

articles.headline: Himachal Pradesh Unveils New Ecotourism Policy to Boost Sustainable Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Himachal Pradesh government launched a new ecotourism policy aimed at promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices.
• The policy focuses on protecting the state's fragile Himalayan ecosystem while generating livelihood opportunities for local communities.
• It encourages private sector investment in ecotourism infrastructure, emphasizing minimal environmental impact and community participation.
• Key initiatives include developing nature trails, adventure tourism circuits, and promoting local culture and handicrafts.
• This move seeks to position Himachal Pradesh as a premier ecotourism destination, balancing conservation with economic development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Himachal Pradesh launched a new ecotourism policy.
Aims to promote sustainable tourism and protect Himalayan ecosystem.
Encourages private investment, community participation.
Focus on nature trails, adventure tourism, local culture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of ecotourism and its importance for sustainable development in ecologically sensitive regions like the Himalayas.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of implementing an ecotourism policy in a state like Himachal Pradesh, balancing conservation and economic needs.
Evaluate the role of local communities and private sector in fostering responsible tourism practices and generating sustainable livelihoods.
Examine how government policies can leverage natural beauty for tourism while ensuring minimal environmental degradation.
articles.imageDescription: A scenic view of the Himalayas with a small village, symbolizing ecotourism.
articles.trivia: Himachal Pradesh is often referred to as 'Dev Bhoomi' (Land of Gods) due to its numerous ancient temples and spiritual sites.

articles.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Adopts Innovative Technologies for River Rejuvenation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the adoption of several innovative technologies for effective river rejuvenation.
• These include advanced wastewater treatment plants, real-time water quality monitoring systems, and AI-powered data analysis for pollution hotspots.
• The focus is on integrating technology with community engagement to achieve sustainable outcomes in cleaning the Ganga and its tributaries.
• New pilot projects for bioremediation and riverine biodiversity conservation were also unveiled, emphasizing holistic ecological restoration.
• This strategic shift aims to accelerate the Namami Gange program's objectives and ensure long-term health of the river system.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: NMCG adopted innovative technologies for Ganga rejuvenation.
Includes advanced STPs, real-time water quality monitoring, AI analysis.
Focus on technology integration with community engagement.
Pilot projects for bioremediation and biodiversity conservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-pronged approach of the Namami Gange program in addressing the pollution of the River Ganga and its ecological restoration.
Analyze the role of innovative technologies, such as AI and advanced wastewater treatment, in accelerating river cleaning efforts and ensuring sustainable outcomes.
Evaluate the challenges faced by large-scale river rejuvenation projects in India and suggest strategies for more effective implementation.
Examine the importance of community participation and public awareness in the success of environmental conservation initiatives like Clean Ganga.
articles.imageDescription: A clean section of the Ganga river with a city in the background, symbolizing rejuvenation.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

highlights: National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II launched, targeting 10 million tonnes annual production by 2030.
Hinduja Group Chairman Gopichand Hinduja passes away at 85, leaving behind a vast business legacy.
Supreme Court directs review of Data Protection Bill provisions concerning state surveillance and individual privacy.
India-ASEAN Summit strengthens digital cooperation and trade ties, pushing for AIFTA review.
ISRO successfully launches Aditya-L2 mission for advanced solar studies from Lagrange Point 1.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Phase II of the mission aims to achieve 10 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030.
2. The mission primarily focuses on incentives for green hydrogen production and manufacturing of electrolysers.
3. It seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and contribute to decarbonization.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 10 million tonnes annual production by 2030, focuses on incentives for production and electrolyser manufacturing, and seeks to reduce fossil fuel reliance and decarbonize the economy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements correctly describes the significance of the Aditya-L2 mission?
(a) It is India's first mission to study Venus's atmosphere.
(b) It is designed to explore the Martian surface for signs of ancient life.
(c) It is India's second dedicated solar observatory, placed at Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1.
(d) It aims to map the entire electromagnetic spectrum of distant galaxies.
options: (a) It is India's first mission to study Venus's atmosphere.
(b) It is designed to explore the Martian surface for signs of ancient life.
(c) It is India's second dedicated solar observatory, placed at Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1.
(d) It aims to map the entire electromagnetic spectrum of distant galaxies.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) It is India's second dedicated solar observatory, placed at Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aditya-L2 is indeed India's second dedicated solar observatory, following Aditya-L1, and is positioned at the Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1) for uninterrupted solar observation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the Supreme Court's ruling on the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, what was the primary concern highlighted by the court?
(a) The lack of provisions for data localization.
(b) Insufficient safeguards against potential state surveillance.
(c) The absence of a dedicated data protection authority.
(d) The high penalties imposed on data fiduciaries.
options: (a) The lack of provisions for data localization.
(b) Insufficient safeguards against potential state surveillance.
(c) The absence of a dedicated data protection authority.
(d) The high penalties imposed on data fiduciaries.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Insufficient safeguards against potential state surveillance.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court primarily directed the government to review provisions that grant broad exemptions to government agencies, emphasizing the need for clearer safeguards against potential state surveillance and citing concerns over the balance between national security and individual privacy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about the 'Skill India Plus' initiative:
1. It is an enhanced version of the existing Skill India Mission.
2. It focuses on upskilling and reskilling in traditional crafts only.
3. Industry-academia partnerships are a key feature of the initiative.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as 'Skill India Plus' is an enhanced version. Statement 3 is correct as industry-academia partnerships are a key feature. Statement 2 is incorrect; the initiative focuses on emerging technologies like AI, IoT, blockchain, and green skills, not just traditional crafts.

prelimsMocks.question:
The newly discovered ancient Buddhist monastery in Odisha's Jajpur district is believed to be part of which historical Buddhist heritage site?
(a) Sanchi Stupa complex
(b) Ajanta-Ellora caves
(c) The 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist heritage
(d) Nalanda University ruins
prelimsMocks.explanation: The newly unearthed Buddhist monastery in Jajpur is believed to be part of the historical 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist heritage in Odisha, which includes Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II aims to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen production. Discuss the strategic imperatives driving this ambition and analyze the economic and environmental benefits India can accrue from a robust hydrogen economy. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the mission's scale and ambition. Discuss strategic imperatives like energy security, decarbonization, and global leadership. Analyze economic benefits (job creation, export potential, new industries) and environmental benefits (reduced emissions, fossil fuel reliance). Conclude on the holistic impact.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's directive to review provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill highlights the perennial tension between national security and individual privacy. Critically examine this balance in the context of emerging digital technologies and suggest measures to strengthen citizen privacy without compromising national security. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—Fundamental Rights; Government policies and interventions; Cyber security challenges
mainsMocks.approach: Start by acknowledging the SC ruling and the inherent conflict. Define 'right to privacy' and 'national security'. Discuss how digital technologies exacerbate this tension (surveillance capabilities, data aggregation). Critically examine the current legal framework. Suggest measures: independent oversight, robust data anonymization, clear legal definitions, judicial warrants for access, public awareness, and technological safeguards.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Act East' policy has gained significant momentum, with the latest India-ASEAN Summit strengthening digital cooperation and trade. Evaluate the evolving contours of India's engagement with ASEAN, focusing on the strategic and economic dimensions, and assess the challenges that lie ahead. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the 'Act East' policy and its evolution from 'Look East'. Discuss strategic dimensions: counterbalancing China, maritime security, regional stability, and multilateralism. Analyze economic dimensions: trade agreements (AIFTA review), digital economy, investment flows, infrastructure connectivity. Assess challenges: China's influence, non-tariff barriers, varying levels of development, and internal political dynamics within ASEAN. Conclude with forward-looking strategies.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to achieve 10 million tonnes of green hydrogen production by 2030.
Gopichand Hinduja was the chairman of the Hinduja Group, a diversified global conglomerate.
The Supreme Court directed review of Digital Personal Data Protection Bill provisions on state surveillance.
Aditya-L2 is ISRO's second dedicated solar observatory, located at Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1.
The Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The 'Make in India' initiative promotes indigenous manufacturing, including in the defence sector.
The 'Diamond Triangle' in Odisha refers to the Buddhist heritage sites of Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri.
The National Commission for Women (NCW) is a statutory body working for women's rights.
Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical regions.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The excavation revealed stupas, monastic cells, statues of Buddhist deities, and intricate carvings dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This finding adds significant new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its architectural and artistic traditions.
• The site is believed to be part of the historical 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist heritage in Odisha, which includes Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri.
• Efforts are underway for further exploration, preservation, and potential development of the site as a heritage tourism destination.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient Buddhist monastery in Jajpur, Odisha.
Dates back to 7th-10th centuries CE.
Revealed stupas, monastic cells, Buddhist deities, carvings.
Adds to the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist heritage in Odisha.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history of Buddhism and its influence on Indian art and culture.
Analyze the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Examine the potential of heritage sites like this to boost cultural tourism and local economic development in regions like Odisha.
Elaborate on the architectural features and iconography typically found in ancient Buddhist monastic complexes in India.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with stupas and statues.
GS1.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Lalitgiri, and Udayagiri) is known for its rich Buddhist archaeological remains, reflecting the growth of Vajrayana Buddhism.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Review of Data Protection Bill Provisions on State Surveillance
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, directed the government to review certain provisions of the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023.
• The court emphasized the need for clearer safeguards against potential state surveillance, citing concerns over the balance between national security and individual privacy.
• The ruling stemmed from petitions challenging clauses that grant broad exemptions to government agencies from data processing obligations.
• The bench highlighted the 'right to privacy' as a fundamental right and mandated a more robust oversight mechanism for data access by state entities.
• This decision is expected to lead to amendments ensuring greater accountability and transparency in state data handling practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Cyber security challenges
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruled on Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023.
Directed review of provisions related to state surveillance exemptions.
Emphasized 'right to privacy' and need for robust oversight.
Impacts government data handling and transparency.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the balance between national security and individual privacy in the digital age.
Discuss the challenges in drafting data protection laws that effectively safeguard citizen rights while allowing for legitimate state functions.
Evaluate the role of judicial review in upholding fundamental rights against legislative overreach in the context of emerging technologies.
Suggest legislative and technological measures to enhance data protection and minimize surveillance risks.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and law books in a courtroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes, Boosting Digital Cooperation and Trade Ties
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to strengthening strategic partnership.
• Key agreements were reached on enhancing digital cooperation, including cybersecurity, digital payments, and developing a regional digital economy framework.
• Discussions also focused on accelerating trade and investment flows, with a renewed push for reviewing the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA).
• Regional security challenges, including maritime security and counter-terrorism, were also addressed, emphasizing collaborative approaches.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic alignment with Southeast Asian nations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Summit concluded with focus on digital cooperation and trade.
Agreements on cybersecurity, digital payments, and regional digital economy.
Discussion on reviewing ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA).
Reaffirms India's 'Act East' policy and strategic partnership.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of India's 'Act East' policy in strengthening ties with ASEAN nations in the current geopolitical scenario.
Discuss the potential of digital cooperation between India and ASEAN in fostering economic growth and addressing regional challenges.
Analyze the role of trade agreements like AIFTA in boosting economic integration and what reforms are needed for its effective implementation.
Examine the shared security concerns between India and ASEAN and strategies for collaborative approaches.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from India and ASEAN nations shaking hands at a summit.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Skill India Plus' Initiative for Future-Ready Workforce
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Skill India Plus', an enhanced version of the existing Skill India Mission.
• This new initiative focuses on upskilling and reskilling the workforce in emerging technologies like AI, IoT, blockchain, and green skills.
• It aims to bridge the skill gap in industries undergoing rapid technological transformation and boost employability among youth.
• Key features include industry-academia partnerships, digital learning platforms, and greater emphasis on apprenticeship opportunities.
• The program targets creating a dynamic and adaptable workforce capable of meeting the demands of the 21st-century global economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Skill India Plus initiative launched by Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
Focus on upskilling/reskilling in emerging technologies (AI, IoT, green skills).
Aims to bridge skill gaps and boost youth employability.
Features industry-academia partnerships and digital learning.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of initiatives like 'Skill India Plus' in leveraging India's demographic dividend and preparing its workforce for future challenges.
Analyze the role of emerging technologies in transforming the job market and the implications for skill development policies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of previous skill development programs and suggest measures for improving the outreach and impact of 'Skill India Plus'.
Examine the importance of industry-academia collaboration and apprenticeship models in creating a job-ready workforce.
GS2.imageDescription: Young people engaged in vocational training using modern technology.
GS2.trivia: The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008 as a not-for-profit public limited company under Section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Bonds Scheme for Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bonds Scheme.
• The committee emphasized the need for greater transparency in political funding and suggested mechanisms to disclose donor identities to ensure accountability.
• Concerns were raised regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on fair electoral practices and corporate influence.
• Recommendations include lowering the minimum denomination of electoral bonds and introducing a digital portal for real-time disclosure of donations.
• The report calls for a comprehensive review of electoral finance laws to enhance public trust in democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Transparency & Accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary panel recommended reforms to Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Suggested disclosing donor identities for greater transparency.
Concerns over anonymity and corporate influence.
Recommendations include lowering denomination and digital disclosure.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bonds Scheme in India, highlighting its objectives and the controversies surrounding it.
Discuss the importance of transparency in political funding for a healthy democracy and the challenges in achieving it.
Evaluate the recommendations made by the Parliamentary Standing Committee and their potential impact on electoral reforms.
Examine alternative models of political funding adopted in other democracies and their applicability to India.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with a focus on governance.
GS2.trivia: Electoral bonds were introduced in 2017 through the Finance Bill, 2017, amending the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

GS2.headline: India and UK Sign Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement
GS2.explainer:
• India and the United Kingdom officially signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) after extensive negotiations.
• The agreement aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment across various sectors, including services, manufacturing, and technology.
• Key provisions include tariff reductions, easier market access, and cooperation on intellectual property rights and sustainable trade practices.
• This CEPA is expected to open new avenues for Indian businesses in the UK market and attract British investments into India.
• Both nations anticipate a substantial increase in bilateral trade, contributing to economic growth and job creation in both economies.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and UK signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA).
Aims to boost bilateral trade and investment.
Includes tariff reductions, market access, IP cooperation.
Expected to increase bilateral trade substantially.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the India-UK CEPA for India's trade diversification and economic growth post-Brexit.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges for key Indian sectors, such as textiles, pharmaceuticals, and services, from this agreement.
Evaluate the role of CEPA in strengthening India's geopolitical and economic ties with major global powers.
Examine how such trade agreements contribute to job creation and technological exchange between partner countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together.
GS2.trivia: The UK is one of the largest investors in India among G7 countries, while India is the second-largest investor in the UK.

GS2.headline: National Commission for Women Proposes Stricter Laws Against Cyberstalking
GS2.explainer:
• The National Commission for Women (NCW) submitted recommendations to the Ministry of Home Affairs for amending existing laws to combat cyberstalking more effectively.
• The proposals include increasing penalties for offenders, expanding the definition of cyberstalking to cover emerging digital harassment forms, and streamlining reporting mechanisms.
• The NCW highlighted the rising incidence of online harassment, particularly against women and children, and its severe psychological and social impact.
• Emphasis was placed on enhancing digital literacy and awareness campaigns to empower victims and prevent such crimes.
• The recommendations aim to create a safer online environment and ensure justice for victims of cybercrimes.
gsSubTopics: Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Cyber security challenges
prelimsPoints: NCW proposed stricter laws against cyberstalking.
Recommendations include increased penalties and broader definition.
Aims to combat online harassment against women and children.
Emphasizes digital literacy and awareness campaigns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing menace of cyberstalking and online harassment in India, particularly its impact on women and vulnerable groups.
Critically analyze the existing legal framework for addressing cybercrimes and the need for reforms as suggested by the NCW.
Examine the multi-faceted approach required, including legal, technological, and social measures, to create a safer online environment.
Evaluate the role of statutory bodies like the NCW in advocating for gender justice and policy changes in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone with digital threat icons hovering around.
GS2.trivia: The Information Technology Act, 2000, is the primary law in India dealing with cybercrime and e-commerce.

GS2.headline: Election Commission of India Launches Nationwide Electoral Roll Digitization Project
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) initiated a massive project to digitize and modernize electoral rolls across the country.
• The project aims to enhance accuracy, accessibility, and transparency of voter lists, reducing errors and ensuring wider participation.
• It involves advanced data analytics, GIS mapping, and integration with Aadhaar (voluntary) for de-duplication and verification.
• The ECI emphasized the importance of stakeholder consultations and robust data security measures to protect voter information.
• This initiative is a significant step towards improving electoral integrity and streamlining the electoral process in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: ECI launched nationwide electoral roll digitization project.
Aims for accurate, accessible, transparent voter lists.
Uses data analytics, GIS mapping, voluntary Aadhaar integration.
Focus on data security and stakeholder consultation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of accurate and updated electoral rolls for the integrity of democratic elections in India.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of digitizing electoral rolls, including issues of data privacy and accessibility for all sections of society.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India in upholding the fairness and transparency of the electoral process through such reforms.
Examine how technology can be leveraged to enhance voter participation and reduce electoral malpractices in a diverse country like India.
GS2.imageDescription: An electronic voting machine (EVM) and voter ID cards, symbolizing electoral process.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a permanent and independent body established by the Constitution of India directly to ensure free and fair elections in the country.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: Phase II Unveiled, India Aims for Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and export capabilities.
• New incentives for green hydrogen production, manufacturing of electrolysers, and development of green hydrogen hubs were announced.
• The mission aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors, and create significant employment opportunities.
• Emphasis is placed on R&D for cost reduction and efficient storage, alongside international collaborations for technology transfer.
• This phase targets achieving 10 million tonnes of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030, positioning India as a global leader.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission approved.
Target: 10 million tonnes annual green hydrogen production by 2030.
Focus on production incentives, electrolyser manufacturing, green hydrogen hubs.
Aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and export.
Evaluate the role of government incentives and R&D in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential for job creation and industrial diversification through the Green Hydrogen Mission.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: Gopichand Hinduja, Chairman of Hinduja Group, Dies at 85
GS3.explainer:
• Gopichand Hinduja, the patriarch and chairman of the global Hinduja Group, passed away at the age of 85.
• He, along with his brothers, significantly expanded the diversified conglomerate founded by their father, Parmanand Deepchand Hinduja.
• The Hinduja Group has interests spanning automotive, oil and gas, banking and finance, IT, power, media, and real estate globally, with a strong presence in India.
• He was instrumental in establishing the group's presence in various sectors, contributing significantly to India's economic landscape through investments and employment.
• His legacy includes building a vast business empire and promoting philanthropic initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Gopichand Hinduja, Chairman of Hinduja Group, passed away.
Hinduja Group is a diversified global conglomerate.
Key sectors include automotive, finance, IT, power, real estate.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of large conglomerates like the Hinduja Group in India's economic development and global integration.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for family-run businesses in maintaining global competitiveness and adapting to changing economic landscapes.
Examine the impact of philanthropic initiatives by Indian business leaders on social development.
GS3.trivia: The Hinduja Group was founded in 1914 in Shikarpur, Sindh (now in Pakistan), and initially traded in carpets, dried fruits, and saffron.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Aditya-L2 Mission for Advanced Solar Studies
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the Aditya-L2 mission, India's second dedicated solar observatory.
• Aditya-L2 aims to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the Sun's atmosphere, solar winds, and coronal mass ejections (CMEs).
• The spacecraft will be placed in a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1), offering an uninterrupted view of the Sun.
• Equipped with advanced payloads, it will study solar dynamics, space weather, and their impact on Earth's communication systems.
• This mission reinforces India's growing capabilities in space science and its commitment to contributing to global scientific knowledge.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Aditya-L2 is India's second dedicated solar observatory.
Will be placed in a halo orbit around Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1).
Aims to study solar atmosphere, solar winds, CMEs, and space weather.
Reinforces ISRO's capabilities in space science.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and significance of India's solar missions, such as Aditya-L1 and Aditya-L2, for understanding space weather and its impact.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to India's scientific prowess and global standing.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to study the Sun and the technological innovations required.
Elaborate on the concept of Lagrange Points and their importance in space-based observatories.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Aditya-L2 spacecraft orbiting the Sun at L1 point.
GS3.trivia: Lagrange Points are positions in space where the gravitational forces of two large bodies (like the Sun and Earth) balance each other, providing stable locations for spacecraft.

GS3.headline: Union Government Unveils New Comprehensive Wetlands Conservation Policy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new national policy aimed at strengthening wetland conservation and management.
• The policy emphasizes a participatory approach, involving local communities and stakeholders in the restoration and sustainable use of wetlands.
• It introduces stricter regulations against encroachment and pollution, alongside provisions for ecological restoration and biodiversity protection.
• Special focus is given to urban wetlands, recognizing their critical role in flood mitigation, water purification, and urban biodiversity.
• The policy aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and aims to expand the network of Ramsar Sites.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New national wetlands conservation policy launched.
Emphasizes participatory approach and stricter regulations.
Focus on urban wetlands and alignment with Ramsar Convention.
Aims to expand Ramsar Sites network.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Critically analyze the features of the new wetlands conservation policy and its potential effectiveness in addressing these threats.
Examine the challenges in implementing participatory conservation models and strategies to overcome them.
Elaborate on India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and how this policy strengthens them.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in 1971 in the Iranian city of Ramsar.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focuses on Inflation Control Amid Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance amidst persistent global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI Governor stated that the focus remains on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The central bank highlighted concerns over core inflation stickiness and geopolitical risks impacting commodity prices.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with future rate decisions heavily dependent on evolving inflation trajectory and global cues.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Focus on inflation control and withdrawing accommodation.
Concerns over core inflation and geopolitical risks.
Sixth consecutive meeting with unchanged rates.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in managing inflation and ensuring economic stability in India.
Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst global economic uncertainties and domestic pressures.
Evaluate the impact of high inflation on various sectors of the Indian economy and the strategies adopted by the government and RBI to mitigate it.
Examine the trade-offs between controlling inflation and promoting economic growth in the context of monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with Indian currency notes in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Modernization Drive: Indian Army Inducts Advanced Indigenous Artillery Systems
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Army formally inducted a new batch of indigenously developed advanced artillery gun systems, bolstering its combat capabilities.
• These systems, designed and manufactured by Indian firms, demonstrate significant progress in 'Make in India' in defense.
• The induction aims to replace aging artillery and enhance firepower, range, and precision in varied terrains, including mountainous regions.
• This move aligns with the government's push for self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in defense production and reducing import dependence.
• Training programs for personnel have commenced to ensure seamless integration and operational readiness of the new equipment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Indian Army inducted new indigenous advanced artillery gun systems.
Part of 'Make in India' and Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence.
Aims to enhance firepower, range, and precision.
Replaces aging equipment and reduces import dependence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous defence production for India's national security and strategic autonomy.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in critical defence technologies and manufacturing.
Evaluate the impact of such modernizations on India's conventional deterrence capabilities and border security.
Examine the economic benefits of the 'Make in India' initiative in the defence sector, including job creation and technological spin-offs.
GS3.imageDescription: An indigenous artillery gun system being test-fired in a desert environment.
GS3.trivia: The Dhanush artillery gun, an upgraded version of the Bofors gun, was the first indigenously produced long-range artillery gun in India.

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Achieve Breakthrough in Low-Cost Carbon Capture Technology
GS3.explainer:
• Scientists at an Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) announced a significant breakthrough in developing a novel, low-cost carbon capture technology.
• The new method utilizes advanced materials and processes that are more energy-efficient and scalable compared to existing technologies.
• This innovation has the potential to drastically reduce the cost of capturing carbon dioxide emissions from industrial sources.
• The technology is crucial for India, a major emitter, to meet its climate targets and transition towards a net-zero economy.
• Further research and pilot projects are underway to demonstrate its commercial viability and widespread application.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: IIT researchers developed a low-cost carbon capture technology.
Utilizes energy-efficient and scalable advanced materials.
Aims to reduce CO2 emissions from industrial sources.
Crucial for India's climate targets and net-zero transition.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technologies in achieving India's climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up CCUS technologies in India, considering cost, infrastructure, and public acceptance.
Evaluate the potential impact of indigenous technological breakthroughs like this on India's industrial decarbonization efforts.
Examine the policy support and investment required to transition from lab-scale innovation to widespread commercial deployment of green technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Laboratory equipment symbolizing scientific research in carbon capture.
GS3.trivia: The first industrial-scale carbon capture plant was built in 1938 at a chemical plant in Texas, USA, to produce CO2 for beverage carbonation.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework to Boost Organic Farming and Agri-Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare introduced a new policy framework aimed at promoting organic farming and increasing agri-exports.
• The policy provides enhanced financial support, technical assistance, and market linkages for farmers transitioning to organic practices.
• It emphasizes certification mechanisms, quality control, and brand building for Indian organic products in domestic and international markets.
